title: Lighting a candle for the women in your line
subtitle: A short ritual for honouring ancestral money pain and releasing it forward.
excerpt: A ten-minute ritual for honouring what the women in your family carried financially and releasing the weight you are still holding for them. You need a candle. The ritual takes ten minutes.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, developed for the ancestral forgiveness theme of Day 12 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Candle rituals draw on a widespread folk practice tradition; this version is shaped for the ancestral money-release context.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want a physical ritual to close out work on the generational money pattern, or when grief or resentment about what the women before you endured is present.

content.text: A ten-minute ritual for honouring the financial difficulties of the women in your line and releasing the weight you have been carrying for them. You need one candle.
One candle. A quiet place. Five to ten minutes undisturbed.

Place the unlit candle in front of you. Think of one woman in your family who had a difficult relationship with money: who didn't have enough, who was afraid, who wasn't allowed to earn or keep. Hold her in mind. You don't need to name her aloud.
I am ready to release the money pain I have been carrying for the women in my line. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow a new relationship with money in my line. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

Light the candle. Picture the flame as the light of what you are choosing for yourself and for whatever comes after you. The women who came before did not have the tools you have. The light is for them, and it is for the line they started that you are continuing differently.
Their strength is in me. Their fear is not.
Say it once out loud. Let the candle burn for a few minutes, then blow it out when you're ready.

Original to homemade.education, developed for the ancestral forgiveness theme in Day 12 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Candle rituals are a widespread folk practice with roots in many traditions; this version is shaped specifically for ancestral money-release work rather than drawn from any single tradition.
